title: Europe oil market Iran Strait impact

messages.content: 帮我调研一下欧洲特别是西欧市场对于本轮美以伊冲突爆发以来（2026年2月底），伊朗封闭霍尔木兹海峡后，原油市场的反应，并给出我相关信息来源

webFetch.content:
## 联网检索范围

- **需要联网的部分**
  - 2026年2月下旬美以对伊行动升级、伊朗宣布/实质封闭霍尔木兹海峡之后：
    - 霍尔木兹海峡通行受阻的时间线与程度（“封闭”“基本停摆”“部分恢复”等表述）。
    - Brent 原油价格、自2月末以来的涨幅区间、关键价位（80、90、100+美元等）的时间节点。
    - 欧洲基准天然气（TTF）价格变动、涨幅和峰值水平。
    - 欧洲/西欧金融市场对能源价格冲击的反应：股指、汇率（欧元、英镑）、债券收益率、通胀预期。
    - 欧盟及成员国在能源安全和市场稳定方面的官方态度或声明（是否认为供应有风险、是否有应急措施）。
    - 市场机构、投行或分析师针对欧洲暴露度、通胀和增长影响的量化评估（例如对通胀多增加多少百分点、对GDP增长影响）。
    - 欧洲炼厂、进口结构方面与中东/霍尔木兹相关的依赖度信息（尤其是前期已从俄罗斯转向海湾地区的背景数据）。
  - 所有上述事实点需要可核对日期（集中在2026年2月底至4月初）。

- **无需联网、留给后续模型处理的内容类型**
  - 对欧洲（特别是西欧）原油和成品油市场反应的系统性梳理与总结。
  - 将油价、天然气价格、金融市场、政策表态等信息整合成一段宏观/策略性分析。
  - 对未来走势的判断、情景推演或建议。
  - 任何需要对不同来源作“谁更可信”判断的地方。
  - 面向特定读者（例如投资人、企业）的定制化结论或行动建议。

---

## 检索摘要

- 检索聚焦于：2026年2月底美以对伊开战及伊朗宣布封闭霍尔木兹海峡后，全球油气价格、欧洲能源价格与金融市场的即时反应，以及欧盟在能源安全上的官方表态。  
- 主要找到：多篇路透社关于“伊朗战争引发数十年来最严重油市危机”“霍尔木兹海峡几乎停摆”“布伦特和欧洲天然气大幅上涨”、以及“欧洲市场在能源冲击下承压”的新闻，以及一篇专门分析伊朗冲突对欧洲经济与通胀影响的路透深度稿。相关报道集中在2026年2月28日至3月中旬，并延续到4月初，时间上与题述时点匹配。

---

## 来源清单

1. **Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（通讯社深度稿）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-02  
   - 说明：分析美以对伊冲突及海湾航运受阻如何推高油气价格，并量化对欧洲通胀与增长的潜在影响。

2. **Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/iran-war-throws-oil-market-into-biggest-crisis-decades-2026-02-28/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（评论/专栏）  
   - 可见日期：2026-02-28  
   - 说明：从全球视角讨论美以对伊打击引发的供应中断风险，对霍尔木兹海峡关键性及油价前景做出描述。

3. **Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-03  
   - 说明：报道伊朗冲突导致霍尔木兹通行被有效关闭数日、全球油气价格飙升，并特别提到欧洲天然气价格及对欧洲、亚洲复苏的风险。

4. **Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://reuters.com/world/middle-east/middle-east-oil-shipping-costs-surge-all-time-high-us-iran-conflict-intensifies-2026-03-02/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-02（文内记载为新加坡 3月3日发布）  
   - 说明：详细描述伊朗宣称关闭霍尔木兹后，油轮和LNG运输费率创新高，且直接提到油气价格跳涨。

5. **Oil settles at highest in over a year for second straight day as Iran crisis escalates, snarls Hormuz flows | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-over-1-iran-crisis-disrupts-middle-east-supply-2026-03-04/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-04  
   - 说明：报道霍尔木兹被有效关闭第五天时油价走势，提到Iraq 被迫减产及各国寻求替代供应。

6. **Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-05  
   - 说明：追踪在霍尔木兹“没有船只通行”的背景下布伦特与WTI 日内大涨，并引用市场人士对油价进一步上涨空间的评论。

7. **Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-16  
   - 说明：记录在部分船只重新通过霍尔木兹后，Brent 和 WTI 从此前近40%的累计涨幅有所回落，同时讨论各国应对高油价的储备释放计划。

8. **Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-04-06  
   - 说明：报道 4 月初霍尔木兹仍大体关闭、仅对部分“友好国家”船只放行背景下，Brent 升至约110美元，以及欧洲继续“丧失桶数”流向亚洲。

9. **European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters**  
   - URL: https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/  
   - 来源类型：媒体（新闻）  
   - 可见日期：2026-03-03  
   - 说明：从股市、汇市和利率市场角度讨论伊朗战争引发的能源冲击对欧洲资产价格的影响，包含对Brent 与欧洲天然气涨幅的量化。

10. **EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters**  
    - URL: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/  
    - 来源类型：媒体（新闻，报道欧委会立场）  
    - 可见日期：2026-03-04  
    - 说明：引述欧盟委员会对伊朗危机导致油气价格急升的担忧、对欧盟供应风险评估及对成员国的通报。

---

## 检索到的事实

- **霍尔木兹海峡与中东供应受阻的时间线与程度**
  - 霍尔木兹海峡被描述为全球约20%原油和大量 LNG 的关键通道；在美以对伊行动中断后，多篇报道指出该水道“有效关闭”或“通行几乎停摆”，导致油气运输陷入停顿。[Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)、[Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://reuters.com/world/middle-east/middle-east-oil-shipping-costs-surge-all-time-high-us-iran-conflict-intensifies-2026-03-02/)
  - 2026-03-03 的报道称，通过霍尔木兹海峡的油轮数量从年初以来日均约24艘骤降至 3 月1日仅4艘，其中3艘为伊朗籍，交通被视为“第四天基本停止”。[Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)
  - 另一篇 2026-03-05 报道称，约有 300 艘油轮“仍被困在霍尔木兹海峡内”，在战争爆发后进出交通几乎完全停滞；伊拉克和卡塔尔已经因为无法出口而被迫“关井”减产。[Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)
  - 2026-03-16 报道提到，“部分油轮”重新通过霍尔木兹海峡，促使 Brent 与 WTI 当日回落约 2.8% 和 5.3%，但文中同时强调自 2 月28日美以首次打击以来 Brent 与 WTI 迄今累计涨幅接近 40%。[Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)
  - 截至 2026-04-06，霍尔木兹“基本仍处于关闭状态”，但有少数来自被伊朗视为“友好国家”的船只获准通行；伊朗同时“拒绝立即重开海峡”的提议。[Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)

- **油价与欧洲天然气价格的反应（自 2026年2月底以来）**
  - 在战争爆发前，Brent 已因紧张局势“数周上升至约70美元/桶”，为 2025年8月以来高位；分析预期战事升级后油价将“出现大幅上涨”。[Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/iran-war-throws-oil-market-into-biggest-crisis-decades-2026-02-28/)
  - 2026-03-02 的说明稿指出，在冲突初期，Brent 期货当天上涨近 8% 至约 78 美元/桶；欧盟基准荷兰 TTF 天然气价格涨约 19% 至 38 欧元/MWh，显著高于欧洲央行此前假设的 29.6 欧元/MWh。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)
  - 2026-03-03 的报道称，Brent 当日收涨 4.7%，报 81.40 美元/桶，为 2025年1月以来最高结算价；欧洲天然气价格当天盘中一度飙升 40%，加上前一日 40% 的涨幅，累计涨幅显著。[Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)
  - 2026-03-04 报道中，Brent 再度收在 81.40 美元/桶，为 2025年1月以来最高水平的连续第二日；文中引用分析认为，若战争持续、霍尔木兹保持封堵，油价可能逼近或突破 100 美元。[Oil settles at highest in over a year for second straight day as Iran crisis escalates, snarls Hormuz flows | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-over-1-iran-crisis-disrupts-middle-east-supply-2026-03-04/)
  - 2026-03-05 报道称，随着冲突扩大及供应中断，美国WTI 当日上涨 8.5% 至 81.01 美元/桶（为 2024年7月以来高位），Brent 上涨近 5% 至 85.41 美元/桶，为连续第五个交易日上涨。[Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)
  - 2026-03-16 的报道显示，在布伦特和WTI于 3 月中旬分别刷新 2022年以来高点后，当天 Brent 回落至 100.21 美元/桶，WTI 回落至 93.50 美元/桶，但自 2 月28日冲突开始以来，两者仍累计上涨近 40%。[Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)
  - 截至 2026-04-06，Brent 报 109.77 美元/桶，WTI 报 112.40 美元/桶；报道指出，这属于“极高水平”，主要因霍尔木兹持续关闭、部分中东产量和炼厂停产以及买方寻求替代供应所致。[Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)
  - 2026-03-03 的欧洲市场报道指出，自前一周五以来，Brent 上涨近 10%，欧洲天然气价格飙升 50%；文中强调，欧元区几乎完全依赖进口油气，对这类冲击高度敏感。[European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)

- **欧洲基准气价与欧盟能源安全立场**
  - 2026-03-04 的报道指出，TTF 前月合约在 3 月3日交易中曾升至 65.79 欧元/MWh，为三年来高点，较前一周“翻倍”；欧盟委员会在与成员国的视频通报中表示“高度关注”油气价格飙升，但认为对欧盟实体供应的即时风险有限，因欧盟当前大部分 LNG 来源于美国，对中东中断的“直接暴露度有限（2025 年仅约 8% LNG 来自卡塔尔）”。[EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)
  - 同一报道指出，截至 3 月初，欧盟天然气储存库约为 30% 满，较上一年同期低约 9 个百分点。欧委会担忧高价环境可能减缓成员国在春夏季补库进度，从而增加下一个供暖季的风险，但“目前不考虑启动紧急措施”。[EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)
  - 2026-03-02 的说明稿补充称，自 2022 年俄乌战争以来，欧洲已大幅转移从俄罗斯的能源依赖，增加从海湾地区的油气、LNG 进口，英国、意大利、比利时和波兰在经霍尔木兹的 LNG 进口方面暴露度较高。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)

- **对欧洲/西欧经济与金融市场的影响描述**
  - 2026-03-02 的欧洲宏观说明中，引用欧洲央行（ECB）的敏感性分析称：若油气价格永久性上升约14%，将使当年欧元区 GDP 增速降低 0.1 个百分点，并推高通胀多达 0.5 个百分点；对次年影响相似，之后逐渐消退。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)
  - 同文援引分析师观点，认为当前油市“供应总体充足”，除非冲突进一步升级并波及沙特等关键生产设施，否则油价大幅突破 80–100 美元区间的概率有限；但这类判断主要面向当时（3月初）的基本面情景。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)
  - 2026-03-03 的金融市场报道指出：  
    - 自冲突加剧以来，欧洲股市承压，投资者担忧能源价格冲击再度推高通胀。  
    - 油价和欧洲天然气价格的大幅上涨“唤起了 2022 年俄乌战争引发能源危机的记忆”，但不同之处在于：欧洲本轮无需再“摆脱单一供应国”（俄罗斯），且冲突发生时冬季取暖需求已回落。  
    - 在利率市场，德国两年期国债收益率上涨 7 个基点，市场已基本不再押注年内欧洲央行降息（此前预期约有 40% 几率）。[European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)
  - 同文亦指出：  
    - 欧元兑美元下跌至 1.1656，为数月低点，并跌至对瑞士法郎的十年新低；市场对欧元的风险偏好从“押注升值”转向“购买欧元贬值保护”。  
    - 英镑兑美元也跌至自前一年底以来低位，英国金边债收益率在通胀担忧下上升，反映市场认为能源价格走高会推迟英国央行降息。[European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)
  - 2026-03-04 的欧委会与成员国通报中，重申高价可能拖慢工业复苏，并强调与 2022 年相比，本轮冲击的“供应量”风险较低，但“价格信号”仍可能对工业和家庭用能构成压力。[EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)

- **欧洲对海湾/霍尔木兹航线的能源依赖结构背景**
  - 在 2026-03-02 的说明文中，援引美国能源信息署和Kpler 数据指出：约 20% 全球原油和大量 LNG 通过霍尔木兹，欧洲在俄气占比下降后对海湾地区 LNG 的依赖上升；在欧洲各国中，英国、意大利、比利时、波兰经该航线进口 LNG 的依存度相对更高。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)
  - 同一来源称，波斯湾同时是丙烷、丁烷和乙烷等 NGL 的重要出口地，欧洲部分取暖、燃料和农业用途也依赖这部分供应。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)

---

## 冲突与缺口

- **关于“是否完全封闭霍尔木兹”的表述差异**
  - 多篇报道在 3 月初使用“交通基本停止”“有效关闭第四/第五天”等措辞，强调霍尔木兹对国际油气贸易几乎中断。[Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)、[Oil settles at highest in over a year for second straight day as Iran crisis escalates, snarls Hormuz flows | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-over-1-iran-crisis-disrupts-middle-east-supply-2026-03-04/)
  - 但 3 月中旬与 4 月初的报道则指出，部分船只在伊朗选择性放行下得以通过海峡，这更多被描述为“多数通行仍被阻断，但完全封锁有所松动”。[Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)、[Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)
  - 这些差异主要反映时间推移和局势演变，而非事实冲突。

- **关于油价“上限”的分析预期已被后续走势部分推翻**
  - 3 月2日的说明稿中，某投行分析认为在供应充足情况下，油价大致被“80美元左右”封顶，只有在更大规模升级（例如沙特基础设施受损）下才可能逼近 100 美元。[Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)
  - 后续报道显示，随着霍尔木兹封锁持续和产量下滑，Brent 在 3 月中旬一度逼近甚至略超 100 美元，到 4 月初接近 110 美元，表明早期“80 美元上限”的判断并未成立。[Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)、[Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)

- **缺口和仍待核实的问题**
  - 目前检索到的主流英文来源以路透为主，对“西欧各具体国家（德国、法国、西班牙等）在实物原油流向、炼厂负荷、进口结构调整”层面的细节报道有限，更多是区域层面的概括。
  - 尚未在高可信来源中找到系统量化“西欧原油现货升水”“欧洲/北海原油对中东原油价差变化”的详细数据，这类信息可能存在于付费市场报告或交易所数据中。
  - 对单个行业（如欧洲炼化、航空、航运）在这轮冲突中的成本变化和对冲行为，目前公开新闻只作零散提及，未见全面分析。
  - 非路透的其他主流媒体（例如 FT、Bloomberg）亦可能有关于欧洲市场细节的补充，但在当前检索结果中未直接呈现；后续如需更细粒度信息，可能需要对这些媒体进行针对性搜索或访问付费内容。

sources.title: Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters
sources.snippet:
Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

An aerial view of the island of Qeshm, separated from the Iranian mainland by the Clarence Strait, in the Strait of Hormuz, December 10, 2023. REUTERS/Stringer/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab

FRANKFURT, March 2 - The U.S.-Israeli military assault on Iran threatens to push up inflation and make a dent in Europe's already meagre economic growth.

The attacks have disrupted commercial shipping in the Gulf, a key source of fuel and petroleum products for Europe. The interruption immediately pushed up the prices of these energy supplies on financial markets.

 Sign up here.

This is likely to raise the cost of fuel and cloud the outlook for the European Central Bank and the Bank of England, potentially forcing the latter to put off any further rate cuts until the fog of war clears.

Here is what you need to know about the economic impact of the Iran conflict on Europe:

## CHOKE-POINT FOR OIL, GAS AND OTHER PRODUCTS

The Strait of Hormuz, located between Oman and Iran, is a key gateway for exports from Gulf states, such as oil, gas and chemicals.

Some 20% of global oil, including from producers Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Iraq, Kuwait and Iran, passes through Hormuz, along with large volumes of liquefied natural gas (LNG) from Qatar.

Having shifted away from Russian energy since its invasion of Ukraine, Europe now relies more heavily on imports from the Gulf region.

Among European countries, Britain, Italy, Belgium and Poland are the most reliant on LNG imports that pass via the Strait of Hormuz, according to data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

The Gulf is also a major exporter of propane, butane and ethane, which are used for heating, fuel and in agriculture, according to data from broker Kpler.

## HIGHER FUEL COSTS FOR EUROPEANS?

More than 200 vessels including oil and liquefied gas tankers have dropped anchor around the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waters as a result of the Iran conflict, shipping data shows.

This has immediately pushed up oil and gas prices. Brent futures were up nearly 8% to $78 a barrel while natural gas on the Dutch market was trading up by 19% at 38 euros per megawatt-hour (MWh).

In its December projection the ECB was assuming a natural gas price of 29.6 euros/MWh and a crude price of $62.5 this year.

The ECB is due to publish new macroeconomic projections on March 19, with a cut-off date for energy prices and other market indicators three weeks prior -- that is this coming Wednesday.

This would imply an upgrade to its energy inflation projections. The ECB may, however, choose to produce several scenarios, like it did at the time of Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

UniCredit argued on Monday that the oil price is likely to be capped at around $80 given plentiful supply, and major escalation, like damage to Saudi oil infrastructure, would be needed for the price to rise towards $100.

## WHAT ELSE IS AFFECTED BEYOND ENERGY SUPPLIES?

The vast flow of commercial goods between Europe and Asia has long gone through the Suez Canal.

Many vessels on that route were rerouted around Africa in late 2023 following attacks in the Red Sea by Yemen's Houthi rebels but, prior to the outbreak of the Iran conflict, shipping companies had been weighing increased use of the critical Asia-Europe trade corridor.

Shipping companies on Sunday again began rerouting vessels around Africa, away from the Suez Canal, potentially boosting freight rates and lifting the cost on imported goods.

## WHAT IS THE IMPACT ON GROWTH AND INFLATION?

The ECB's own projections show that the inflation impact of the oil price spike is far greater than the hit to growth.

Its sensitivity analysis published in December argues that a permanent, 14% jump in the oil and gas price would lower growth by just 0.1% this year and raise inflation up to 0.5%.

These impacts would be on a similar magnitude next year, then start to fade.

The euro zone and UK economies were seen growing at 1.2% and 1% respectively this year, and 1.4% each next year, according to Reuters polls. This is a modest pace in comparison to the United States, where output is seen increasing by 2.5% and 2.0% in 2026-27.

But the hit would be tiny compared to the shock of 2022, when Russia's attack on Ukraine pushed up energy costs. That lowered growth by 1 percentage point and lifted inflation by 2 percentage points, according to a European Commission study.

A relatively strong euro would also likely dampen the hit, since energy is priced in dollars.

The growth hit is also likely to temporary as the economy can adjust, so there is no lasting impact on potential output, the ECB argued in a separate study.

## HOW WILL THE CENTRAL BANKS REACT?

Investors trimmed their bets on the Bank of England cutting its benchmark Bank Rate by a quarter of a percentage point later this month with pricing suggesting a 69% probability, down from 78% on Friday.

Do not expect any quick action from the ECB, which was already seen keeping its rates steady for the remainder of this year.

The euro zone's central bank does not react to short term market volatility and also ignores transitory energy price spikes.

So any reaction will depend on how long the conflict lasts and how far it widens. U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday the Iran operation could last four weeks.

Economists at Commerzbank saw no significant impact if the war lasted only a few weeks.

But if it were to drag on for several months, they estimated inflation in the euro zone would probably rise by at least 1 percentage point and economic growth would be a few tenths of a percentage point lower.

Moreover, euro zone inflation, now at 1.7%, is below target, so a modest rise would not jeopardize its objective.

The ECB normally gets worried if a one-off inflation shock starts impacting longer-term price expectations and seeps into broader wage and price setting trends via so-called second round impacts.

This would take many months to materialise, so expect the ECB for now to say that it looks through temporary volatility but remains alert to developments.

Markets-based expectations for longer term inflation are broadly unchanged, likely reinforcing the bank's wait-and-see message.

Markets are on the same page. No interest rate change is priced at all for this year.

Reporting by Francesco Canepa and Balazs Koranyi; Editing by Toby Chopra

Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles., opens new tab

SINGAPORE, March 3 (Reuters) - Supertanker costs in the Middle East have hit ​all-time highs, according to shipping data and industry sources on Tuesday, as the U.S.-Iran conflict intensifies with Tehran attacking ships passing through the ‌Strait of Hormuz.

Shipping through the Strait between Iran and Oman, which carries around one-fifth of oil consumed globally as well as large quantities of liquefied natural gas, has ground to a near halt after vessels in the area were hit as Iran retaliated against U.S. and Israeli strikes.

 Sign up here.

The disruption and fears of prolonged closure ​have caused oil and European natural gas prices to jump, with Brent crude futures up nearly 10% this week as ​the conflict triggered multiple oil and gas shutdowns in the Middle East.

The benchmark freight rate for the very ⁠large crude carriers (VLCCs) used to ship 2 million barrels of oil from the Middle East to China, also known as TD3, ​rose to an all-time high of W419 on Monday on the Worldscale industry measure used to calculate freight rates, or $423,736 per day, LSEG data ​showed.

Mideast to Asia oil shipping cost hit all-time high amid US-Iran conflict

The rate had doubled from Friday, extending gains from a six-year high hit last week, after the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran and killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei on Saturday.

Iran has responded by attacking Gulf countries, prompting precautionary shutdowns at oil and gas facilities across the Middle East.

An Iranian Revolutionary Guards senior official said on Monday that the Strait ​of Hormuz was closed and Iran would fire on any ship trying to pass, Iranian media reported.

## LNG SHIPPING RATES JUMP

Daily freight rates for LNG ​tankers jumped more than 40% on Monday after Qatar halted production.

Item 1 of 2 An aerial view of the island of Qeshm, separated from the Iranian mainland by the Clarence Strait, in the Strait of Hormuz, December 10, 2023. REUTERS/Stringer/File Photo

[1/2]An aerial view of the island of Qeshm, separated from the Iranian mainland by the Clarence Strait, in the Strait of Hormuz, December 10, 2023. REUTERS/Stringer/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab

Atlantic rates rose to $61,500 per day on Monday, up 43%, or $18,750, from Friday, according ‌to Spark ⁠Commodities, a pricing assessment agency for LNG shipping. Pacific rates rose to $41,000 per day, up 45%, or $12,750, from Friday.

Fraser Carson, principal analyst for global LNG at energy consultancy Wood Mackenzie, said spot daily LNG shipping rates could rise above $100,000 this week on tight supply.

"Vessel availability for the rest of March is considered weak as cargo operators try to work through the backlog created by weather disruptions ​during February," he said.

"There will ​be very strong competition for ⁠any available vessels."

Until safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz can be assured, shipping will remain idle, Carson said.

An oil shipbroker who declined to be named due to company policy said it was very ​difficult to assess shipping rates in the Gulf as several shipowners had suspended operations indefinitely.

Meanwhile, bunker sales at ​Fujairah, a major ⁠bunkering port in the United Arab Emirates, have slowed as the conflict disrupted fuel supply, spurring a jump in prices and potentially shifting demand to other ports including Singapore.

South Korean shipping firm Hyundai Glovis (086280.KS), opens new tab said on Tuesday it was preparing contingency plans including securing alternative routes and ports.

South ⁠Korea's maritime ​ministry issued a notice to South Korean shippers with vessels sailing in the ​Middle East, asking them to refrain from business operations in the region, an official told Reuters on Tuesday.

sources.title: Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters
sources.snippet:
Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

Iranian flag with stock graph and an oil pump jack miniature model are seen in this illustration taken October 9, 2023. REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab

LONDON, Feb 28 (Reuters) - Global energy markets face one of their gravest shocks in decades as joint U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran and Tehran’s retaliatory missile attacks across the Gulf disrupt oil exports from the world’s most important producing region.

The scale of the disruption will likely be determined by the duration of the conflict, but for now the threat and the uncertainty are already enough to severely impact flows from the region that accounts for 20% of global oil supplies.

 Sign up here.

Barring a swift resolution, oil prices will likely see steep increases when trading opens on Monday morning. Benchmark Brent crude oil prices rose in recent weeks to around $70 a barrel, their highest since August 2025 as investors braced for military confrontation in the Middle East.

The United States and Israel carried out military strikes on Iran on Saturday, targeting senior leaders and plunging the Middle East into a widening conflict. U.S. President Donald Trump said the attacks would eliminate a security threat to the United States and give Iranians an opportunity to topple their rulers.

For now, there is no confirmed damage to oil and gas infrastructure from retaliatory Iranian strikes.

Explosions were reported in the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait, two major oil exporters. Meanwhile, Qatar, the world’s second-largest exporter of liquefied natural gas, said it intercepted missiles aimed at the country.

Blasts were also heard in Bahrain and near Iran’s Kharg Island, the terminal through which about 90% of its crude exports normally flow, although shipping data suggests Tehran had transferred most of the oil stored there onto tankers in recent days.

Crucially, there have so far been no reports of disruptions to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway between Iran and Oman that handles nearly 20 million barrels per day of crude oil and refined products.

Middle East oil exports

## CAUTION MEANS DISRUPTION

But the absence of physical damage may not matter much.

The risk that tankers could be stranded inside the Gulf, north of Hormuz, or that vessels could be targeted, is already enough to force producers, traders and shippers to rethink movements of oil and LNG. Reuters has reported that some oil majors and trading houses have suspended shipments through the strait for several days.

That caution is unlikely to ease until there is far greater confidence in the safety of the region’s sea lanes.

Tanker freight rates, which had already been climbing as tensions escalated, are set to rise further. Benchmark rates for very large crude carriers from the Middle East to China have more than tripled since the start of the year, reflecting both heightened risk and the shrinking pool of willing vessels.

The key questions now are whether energy infrastructure will be directly targeted and how quickly the U.S. military can secure shipping routes across the Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz.

It is worth noting that the Strait of Hormuz has never been fully blocked.

While Iran is unlikely to sustain a prolonged blockade, it has the capability to disrupt traffic temporarily. The U.S. Navy would almost certainly respond swiftly, but even short-lived attacks or mine-laying operations could have outsized effects on prices and supply.

Such tactics would not be unprecedented. During the 1980s Iran-Iraq war, Iran attacked commercial shipping and U.S. naval vessels, prompting President Ronald Reagan to deploy U.S. forces to escort tankers in Operation Earnest Will. More recently, in late 2007 and early 2008, there were repeated confrontations between Iranian and U.S. naval forces. And in April 2023, Iran's navy seized the Advantage Sweet crude tanker, chartered by Chevron, in the Gulf of Oman. The vessel was released more than a year later.

## GLOBAL SUPPLY CUSHION

The global oil market is relatively well supplied today, after production from the United States, Brazil, Canada and other countries rose in recent years.

Saudi Arabia, the world’s top oil exporter, has also not sat idle in the face of the risk to supply. In recent days the kingdom increased crude shipments, which are set to exceed 7 million barrels per day in February, the highest since April 2023, according to shipping analytics firm Kpler.

OPEC+, which comprises the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allies like Russia, is expected to agree on an output increase during a meeting on Sunday.

Of course, disruptions to export routes from the Middle East could negate much of the production increases from regional producers, though Saudi and the UAE have some alternative export routes.

The scale of the U.S. and Israeli strikes, and Trump’s language, suggest Washington is bracing for a sustained military campaign aimed at severely weakening Iran’s leadership.

How threatened Iran's leadership feels may determine whether it escalates further by attacking a broader range of targets across the region, including oilfields, export terminals and processing facilities.

But even without that worst-case scenario, the conflict is already set to disrupt vital energy supplies from the Middle East in ways not seen for decades.

(The views expressed here are those of the author, a columnist for Reuters)

Writing by Ron Bousso; Editing by Lisa Shumaker

sources.title: Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters
sources.snippet:
Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

Tankers are seen off the coast of the Fujairah, as Iran vows to close the Strait of Hormuz, amid the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran, in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, March 3, 2026.... Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab Read more

- Summary
- Companies

- Hormuz shut for fourth day, five tankers hit
- US gasoline prices rise above $3/gallon
- Bessent, Wright to announce US plans to mitigate oil spike
- Iraq cuts oil production
- Saudi Aramco seeks alternative routes

LONDON, March 3 (Reuters) - Global oil and gas prices jumped on Tuesday as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran halted energy exports from the ​Middle East, with Tehran attacking ships and energy facilities, closing navigation in the Gulf and forcing production stoppages from Qatar to Iraq.

The benchmark Brent crude oil contract settled up $3.66, ‌or up 4.7%, at $81.40 a barrel, its highest settlement since January 2025. European gas prices soared as much as 40% before paring gains, adding to a 40% surge on Monday. Sugar, fertilizer and soy prices have all risen too.

The conflict risks triggering a spike in inflation that could choke off economic recovery in Europe and Asia if the war is prolonged in a region that accounts for just under a third of global oil production and almost a fifth of natural gas. Price rises could ​also pose a political threat to U.S. President Donald Trump and his Republicans in November midterm elections.

Trump said the U.S. Navy could begin escorting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, ​a crucial oil shipping lane, if necessary, adding he had ordered the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation to provide political risk insurance and financial guarantees for ⁠maritime trade in the Gulf. It was one of the administration’s most aggressive steps to try to contain soaring energy prices as conflict escalates in the Middle East.

Iraq, OPEC's second-largest producer, said it may ​be forced to cut production in a few days by more than 3 million barrels per day if oil tankers cannot move freely to loading points, according to two Iraqi oil officials.

As of Tuesday, Iraq has ​decreased production from the Rumaila oil field by 700,000 bpd and cut 460,000 bpd from the West Qurna 2 field, the officials said.

## SHIPPING AT A STANDSTILL, OIL AND GAS OUTPUT SLASHED

Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed for a fourth day after Iran attacked five ships, choking off the artery accounting for about 20% of global oil and LNG supply.

Crude tanker transits through the strait fell to four vessels on March 1, the day after hostilities ​broke out, versus an average of 24 per day since January, according to Vortexa vessel-tracking data. Three of the four were Iran-flagged.

Hundreds of tankers loaded with oil and LNG are stranded near big ​hubs, such as the United Arab Emirates' port of Fujairah, unable to reach customers in Asia, Europe and elsewhere.

Some companies are seeking alternative routes.

Saudi oil giant Aramco 2223.SE, opens new tab is attempting to reroute some crude to its western Red ‌Sea port of ⁠Yanbu, but sources, including buyers, traders and analysts, said Aramco's east-west pipeline had limited capacity and could become a target of attacks by Iran's allies.

On Tuesday, a fuel tank at Oman’s Duqm commercial port was hit by a drone and a fire broke out at the UAE's Fujairah, one of the key regional oil hubs, slowing ship refueling and potentially shifting demand to other ports including Singapore.

On Monday, Qatar shut its liquefied natural gas facilities, some of the world's biggest, which supply around 20% of global LNG exports. Saudi Arabia suspended production at its largest domestic refinery, while Israel and Iraq's Kurdistan also shut chunks of their gas ​and oil output.

Elsewhere, Chinese refiners have started to shut ​units in response to the conflict's impact on ⁠crude supply. India, one of the most dependent countries on oil and gas from the Middle East, has said it has started to ration gas supplies to industries after Qatar production was shut down.

## RISING GASOLINE PRICES POSE POLITICAL RISKS

In the U.S., where gasoline prices are a key political pressure point, the cost jumped ​above$3 per gallon for the first time since November, just weeks after Trump touted his achievements in bringing prices down to $2.

Higher prices at ​the pump mark a major ⁠risk for Trump's Republicans ahead of midterm elections.

Most Qatari LNG flows to Asia, but some also flows to Europe, which depends entirely on imported oil and gas. Europe is expected to scramble to replenish stocks, depleted by a cold winter, and will need to rely more on U.S. gas, after shunning Russian gas after its 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

Shipping rates have jumped to an all-time high as the conflict has intensified and Tehran ⁠has targeted ships ​passing through the strait.

## ASSESSING MISSILE STOCKPILES

Western security experts are seeking to assess how many missiles and drones Iran has left ​to keep up the intensity of its attacks.

Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Oman and Kuwait have so far managed to intercept most missiles and drones targeting energy facilities, ports and airports but worries mount if their anti-drone and anti-missile stockpiles are running low.

Reporting ​by Yousef Saba, Ahmed Elimam; additional reporting by Ahmad Ghaddar and Alex Lawler in London and Nidhi Verma in Delhi; Writing by Dmitry Zhdannikov and Nina Chestney; Editing by Sharon Singleton and David Gregorio

He previously covered Gulf financial and economic news, with a focus on the fast-growing capital markets there. He joined Reuters in 2018 in Cairo, where he covered Egypt and Sudan, including its uprising. European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters

- Summary

- Make Europe Great Again trades could resurface - analyst
- Markets dial back ECB, BoE rate-cut bets
- Euro hits lowest level against Swiss franc since 2015

LONDON, March 3 (Reuters) - European financial markets are under strain as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran revives concerns about an energy supply shock exacerbating ​inflation.

ING says the euro zone is the most exposed major economy to the conflict, making the region, which has benefited from investor diversification out of U.S. assets, vulnerable to setbacks.

Sign up here.

## NOT GOOD, ‌BUT 2022 WAS WORSE

The jump in oil and gas prices evokes memories of Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022, which triggered a global energy crunch and hit Europe particularly hard.

Since Friday, Brent crude is up nearly 10%, while European natural gas prices have shot up 50%. The region is almost entirely dependent on imports for oil and gas.

Major liquefied natural gas exporter QatarEnergy said on Monday it had halted production, while global oil and gas shipping rates have soared.

But, unlike 2022, European buyers don't have to wean themselves off ​one major energy supplier, as they did with Russia and this conflict has erupted as winter heating demand ebbs.

Furthermore, the euro is still some 4% higher than in February 2022 . So, barring a ​spike in the dollar, euro strength helps limit energy import bills. By contrast, other big importers like Japan, South Korea have seen their currencies weaken.

A rebasement chart of the oil price in different currencies over the last six months.

## INFLATION, NOT AGAIN

For interest ⁠rates, it's all about the impact of energy prices on inflation.

The immediate outlook for the European Central Bank hasn't changed. But traders now see no change in rates by year-end, compared with around a 40% chance of a ​cut last week.

Rate-sensitive German two-year bond yields rose 7 basis points on Monday.

The ECB expects inflation to undershoot its 2% target this year and next, so it has some leeway. It has calculated, however, that a permanent ​14% jump in energy prices would lower growth by 0.1% this year and raise inflation by up to 0.5%.

Oil prices are over 20% higher than its December forecast, which it refreshes on March 19.

A sustained rise in oil to around $100 would raise inflation to just under 3% from 1.7% today, Commerzbank's Chief Economist Joerg Kraemer said, adding that would hurt euro zone growth, posing a "dilemma" for the ECB.

Inflation across the Group of Seven industrialized economies

## EURO TRASHED

A prolonged war could cause a substantial spike in euro zone inflation and reduce economic growth, ECB ​Chief Economist Philip Lane told the Financial Times in an interview published on Tuesday.

Such fears have hit the euro, a big faller among developed market currencies. The euro declined 0.3% to $1.1656 on Tuesday, its lowest since January, ​and is now down 1.4% since Friday's close.

It also hit an over-10-year low on the Swiss franc that triggered threats from authorities they might intervene to weaken the franc , .

It could fall further if the economy gets hit by higher energy prices, and ‌investors reverse ⁠previous bets on euro appreciation.

JPMorgan said if Brent crude reaches $100-$120, which might happen if the conflict lasts more than three weeks, the euro could weaken to $1.10-$1.13.

Sharp shifts in three-month risk reversals, a derivative product, are also telling. Investors are paying a small premium to insure against euro depreciation. A month ago, the cost of protecting against euro appreciation was its highest in nine months.

Chart shows three month euro dollar risk reversals

## BRITISH MARKETS FEEL A STING

Sterling touched its lowest against the dollar since December , while gilt yields jumped on concerns that rising oil prices will feed into inflation .

The Bank of England estimates that a 10% rise in the price of Brent crude adds around 0.2 to 0.3 percentage points to UK inflation.

While ​trending down, Britain still has the highest inflation in ​the Group of Seven industrialised economies and it's ⁠no surprise that traders have trimmed March BoE rate cut bets .

"Although you could say that (the change in rate cut bets) is sterling supportive in the short term, the reality is higher energy prices in the UK at a time when taxes have gone up would have a very negative business impact, growth impact, and political impact," ​said Rabobank head of FX strategy Jane Foley.

Sterling weakest performing major currency so far this year

## BANKS UNDER PRESSURE

European banks have been hit hard this week, extending losses made on Friday due to concerns about private ​credit.

European banks (.SX7P), opens new tab shed almost 5% ⁠over Monday and Friday, their biggest two-day drop since last April's tariff turmoil.

"It's very classic risk-off. Just broad selling of equities across the board," said Marlborough portfolio manager Rory Dowie.

While European banks do have counterparty exposure in the Middle East, the European Banking Authority's, opens new tab 2024 risk assessment noted this made up only a fraction of the EU/EEA banking sector's total.

Column chart showing two-day percent gain/fall of STOXX Europe 600 Banks Index

## MAKE EUROPE GREAT AGAIN TRADES

Some noted a silver lining to the selloff, should U.S. policy strengthen Europe's push to invest in ⁠defense and infrastructure. ​That would boost long-term growth.

"The European story is underappreciated and growth will surprise on the upside this year," said Lloyds FX strategist ​Nick Kennedy.

"The strikes on Iran are also a reminder of the tricky nature of dealing with Trump and galvanise that approach to invest more in defense and become more independent."

Chart shows outperformance by STOXX 600 over S&P 500

EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

Model of natural gas pipeline and EU flag, July 18, 2022. REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab

- Summary

- Gas prices spike as Iran war disrupts LNG flows, shipping
- Brussels urges vigilance, says EU supplies remain secure
- High prices risk slowing gas storage refills ahead of winter

BRUSSELS, March 4 (Reuters) - The European Union has warned ‌member states about soaring gas prices caused by the Iran crisis, but saw no immediate threat to supplies and was not planning an emergency response, officials told Reuters on Wednesday.

Global oil and gas markets ​have been jolted by the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran and Iran's attacks across the ​Middle East, halting Qatari LNG production and energy shipments through the Strait ⁠of Hormuz.

 Sign up here.

Europe's benchmark gas price, the Dutch front-month contract at the TTF hub , eased ​slightly on Wednesday, having surged to three-year highs during trading on Tuesday - peaking at 65.79 ​euros/MWh, more than double prices last week.

The European Commission told EU members on Wednesday on videocalls that it was concerned about soaring oil and gas prices, but said there was no immediate risk to ​EU supplies, four officials familiar with the discussions said.

The Commission said, for now, the ​EU was not planning emergency measures in response.

## HIGH PRICES MAY AFFECT STORAGE FOR WINTER

Brussels is concerned that ‌high ⁠prices could stall EU countries refilling their gas storage in the coming months. EU countries are obliged to fill their storage to 90% of capacity by November to provide a buffer of fuel for winter.

EU gas storage is currently 30% full, around 9% below ​levels this time last ​year, data from ⁠Gas Infrastructure Europe showed.

One of the officials said Brussels had observed no major storage withdrawals in the last few days.

Europe now gets ​most of its liquefied natural gas from the United States, reducing ​its direct ⁠exposure to supply disruptions in the Middle East. The EU imported 8% of its LNG from Qatar last year.

But governments have been on edge about the Iran conflict's impact on energy ⁠prices, following ​Europe's 2022 energy price surge to record peaks ​after Russia invaded Ukraine and slashed gas supplies to Europe. That forced industries to shutter and prompting government interventions ​to shield consumers from soaring bills.

A cargo ship in the Gulf, near the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from northern Ras al-Khaimah, near the border with Oman’s Musandam governance, amid the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, in United... Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab Read more

- Summary

- Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts oil exports, refiners seek alternatives
- Trump threatens further action if strait remains closed
- Iran allows passage for select vessels
- OPEC+ agrees on modest output rise, war limits actual increases
- Russian supply also disrupted

HOUSTON, April 6 (Reuters) - Oil prices climbed in choppy trade on Monday, as the U.S. and Iran ratcheted up their rhetoric ​even as the two countries are engaging in indirect talks that could lead to the de-escalation of hostilities.

Brent crude futures settled at $109.77 ‌a barrel, up 74 cents, or 0.68%. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures settled at $112.40, up 87 cents or 0.78%.

For prices to decline to less exorbitant levels, any cessation of attacks would need to come with an agreement to open the crucial Strait of Hormuz, the shipping artery used by one-fifth of the world's oil and gas supply. Major oil consumers, ​particularly in Asia, are conserving barrels or cutting consumption in response to the closure of the strait.

The U.S. and Iran received a framework from ​Pakistan to end hostilities, but Iran rejected the idea of immediately reopening the strait after President Donald Trump threatened to ⁠rain "hell" on the nation if it did not make a deal by the end of Tuesday.

The strait, which carries oil and petroleum products from Iraq, Saudi ​Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates, remains largely closed due to Iranian attacks on shipping after the U.S.-Israel attacks began on February 28.

Some vessels, however, including ​an Omani-operated tanker, a French-owned container ship and a Japanese-owned gas carrier, have passed through the strait since Thursday, shipping data showed, reflecting Iran's policy to allow passage for vessels from countries it deems friendly.

"The market is trying to realise what to expect going forward. The most important headline this weekend has been that some ships passed through the strait," ​said SEB Research analyst Ole Hvalbye.

Hvalbye also highlighted that Europe continued to lose physical barrels and products to Asia due to the market tightening.

Iran said ​it had formulated its positions and demands in response to recent ceasefire proposals conveyed via intermediaries.

"It's a very fluid situation with peace plans being put out there," said John Kilduff, ‌a partner ⁠with Again Capital. "The rhetoric out of Iran seems to reject a ceasefire proposal, but they are allowing more ships through the Strait of Hormuz."

Monday's price moves followed an 11% surge for WTI and an 8% rise for Brent during the previous trading session on Thursday, the biggest absolute price increase since 2020.

## SEEKING ALTERNATIVE SOURCES

The Middle East supply disruptions have led to refiners seeking alternative sources for crude, particularly for physical cargoes in the U.S. and Britain's North Sea. Spot ​premiums for U.S. WTI crude have ​jumped to all-time highs on competition between ⁠Asian and European refiners.

Indian refiners have also postponed maintenance shutdowns of their units to meet local fuel demand.

On Sunday, OPEC+, consisting of some members of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allies such as Russia, agreed to a modest rise ​of 206,000 barrels per day for May.

Russian supply has been disrupted recently by Ukrainian drone attacks on its Baltic Sea export terminals. Media reports on Sunday said its Ust-Luga ⁠terminal resumed loadings ​on Saturday after days of disruptions.

Exports from the Black Sea port of Tuapse are set to ​rise to 794,000 metric tons in April, up 8.7% on a daily basis from 755,000 metric tons planned for March, according to two traders and Reuters calculations.

Oil settles at highest in over a year for second straight day as Iran crisis escalates, snarls Hormuz flows | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

Flames rise from a gas flare at the Rumaila oil field, as the country cuts nearly 1.5 million barrels per day of output amid halted exports following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, in... Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab Read more

- Summary
- Companies

- Brent settles at its highest since January 2025
- US working on plans to secure the Strait of Hormuz
- US crude stocks rise by 3.5 million barrels, EIA says

HOUSTON, March 4 (Reuters) - Oil prices settled unchanged on Wednesday at the end of a volatile trading session, as escalating U.S. and ​Israeli strikes against Iran widened regional tensions and paralysed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz for a fifth day, disrupting vital Middle ‌East oil and gas flows.

Brent crude settled at $81.40 per barrel, flat to Tuesday's close and at its highest since January 2025. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude settled 10 cents, or 0.1%, higher at $74.66, closing at its highest since June for the second day in a row.

 Sign up here.

"Oil prices remain elevated as markets grapple with the prospect of a prolonged ​war and lingering supply disruptions," Nikos Tzabouras, Senior Market Analyst at Tradu.com said.

"The U.S. has signalled a four- to five-week campaign, Iran ​is seeking to regionalise the conflict and the crucial Strait of Hormuz chokepoint is effectively shut. These developments could ⁠overturn previously unfavourable supply-demand dynamics, pushing crude higher and bringing the $100 threshold into view," Tzabouras added.

The Brent benchmark had gained more than $3 to touch $84.48 ​in morning trading, within sight of multi-month highs, but traded lower after the New York Times reported operatives from Iran's Ministry of Intelligence signalled openness to ​the U.S. Central Intelligence Agency to talks on ending the war, citing officials briefed on the matter.

On Wednesday, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the U.S. was winning the war against Iran and that the U.S. military could fight as long as needed.

Israeli and U.S. forces have struck targets across Iran, prompting Iranian retaliatory strikes against energy infrastructure in a ​region that accounts for just under a third of global oil production.

Iraq, the second-largest crude producer in the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, has cut ​output by nearly 1.5 million barrels a day due to storage limits and the lack of an export route, officials told Reuters.

They said the country may have to ‌shut nearly ⁠3 million bpd of output within days if exports do not resume. Traffic through the Strait also remains effectively closed.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday said the U.S. Navy could begin escorting oil tankers through the strait if necessary, adding that he had ordered the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation to provide political risk insurance and financial guarantees for maritime trade in the Gulf.

The Pentagon and the U.S. Energy Department are working on plans to secure the ​Strait of Hormuz to ensure safety for ​oil tankers amid the war ⁠on Iran, White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said on Wednesday, adding that Trump and his advisors were also discussing what role the U.S. could have in Iran after the military campaign.

Countries and companies have, meanwhile, begun seeking alternative routes ​and supplies of crude. India and Indonesia said they were looking for other supplies, while some Chinese refineries ​were shutting or moving up ⁠maintenance plans.

In the U.S., crude stocks rose by 3.5 million barrels in the last week to their highest in three and a half years, the Energy Information Administration said, compared with analysts' expectations in a Reuters poll for a 2.3 million-barrel rise. U.S. gasoline stocks fell by 1.7 million barrels, while distillate stockpiles, ⁠which include ​diesel and heating oil, rose by 429,000 barrels in the week.

"Global supplies remain ample with ​near record levels of “on the water” tanker storage. Still, until that oil can find a safe home, look for price volatility to continue," said Dennis Kissler, senior vice president of trading ​at BOK Financial.

Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

- Summary

- Trump urges nations to help unblock Strait of Hormuz
- WTI prices fall due in part to U.S. crude output and reserve releases
- IEA may release more oil to combat price spikes

NEW YORK, March 16 (Reuters) - Oil prices slid about 3% on Monday after some vessels sailed through the critical Strait ​of Hormuz, even as U.S. allies rebuffed President Donald Trump's call for help in unblocking the strait, and as the head of ‌the IEA suggested more reserves could be released to stem the rising costs caused by the Iran war.

Brent futures fell $2.93, or 2.8%, to settle at $100.21 a barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude fell $5.21, or 5.3%, to settle at $93.50.

 Sign up here.

Analysts said U.S. prices fell by more than Brent due to several reasons, including near-record U.S. crude output bolstered by Venezuela imports and ​the upcoming release of oil from the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve. In addition, some traders were selling the April WTI front-month contract before ​its coming expiration on the New York Mercantile Exchange on March 20.

On Friday, Brent closed at its highest since August ⁠2022 and WTI at its highest since July 2022, putting both benchmarks up almost 40% since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

Trump repeated his ​call for nations to help unblock the Strait of Hormuz, and complained that other countries were not enthusiastic about providing aid.

European Union foreign ministers currently have "no appetite" ​to expand an EU naval mission in the Middle East to the strait, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said on Monday.

The Strait of Hormuz is a critical shipping waterway for a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) supplies.

Iran, which has allowed some Indian vessels to sail through the Strait of Hormuz, asked India to release three tankers seized in ​February as part of talks seeking the safe passage of Indian‑flagged or India‑bound vessels through the strait, three sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

A drone view of a pump jack and drilling rig south of Midland, Texas, U.S. June 11, 2025. REUTERS/Eli Hartman//File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab

"The (oil) ​complex is selling off ... on reports that some oil tankers are proceeding through the Strait of Hormuz and as Trump appeals for help in escorting tankers through the strait," ‌analysts at ⁠energy advisory firm Ritterbusch and Associates said in a note.

Earlier Monday, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the United States is "fine" with some Iranian, Indian and Chinese ships going through the Strait of Hormuz for now, adding that any action to mitigate higher prices would depend on how long the war lasts.

## SOARING ENERGY COSTS

Governments worldwide are trying to shield consumers from soaring energy costs as the disruption to global oil and gas supplies caused by the war ripples through economies.

Member countries of ​the International Energy Agency(IEA) could release more oil ​into the market from strategic stockpiles "as ⁠and if needed" after they agreed to the largest-ever release of 400 million barrels last week, Executive Director Fatih Birol said on Monday.

Israel said it has detailed plans for at least three more weeks of war as its military pounded sites ​across Iran overnight. U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Sunday he expected an end to the war within "the next ​few weeks," with oil ⁠supplies rebounding and energy costs falling afterwards.

Over the weekend, Trump threatened further strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, which handles about 90% of the country's exports, after hitting military targets there that spurred further retaliation from Tehran. The U.S. is in contact with Iran, Trump said.

Abu Dhabi state oil giant ADNOC has suspended crude loading operations at the United ⁠Arab Emirates , a source familiar with the situation told Reuters on Monday, after a drone ​attack triggered fires at the key export terminal.

However, two other sources said some loading at Fujairah restarted. Two of the three single-point moorings, where tankers connect to load, were operational, one source said.

sources.snippet:
Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters

Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv

3D-printed oil pump jacks, Iranian flag, and a rising stock graph appear in this illustration taken March 2, 2026. REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab

- Summary

- US-Israeli conflict with Iran disrupts oil supplies, shipping
- Iraq cuts output by 1.5 million bpd due to storage, export issues
- Attacks on oil tankers in Gulf, Chinese fuel export cuts push prices higher

HOUSTON, March 5 (Reuters) - Oil ​prices rallied on Thursday on growing disruption to global oil supplies caused by the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, with U.S. futures prices ‌rising faster than the international benchmark Brent futures as Washington said it may take action in the futures market to combat rising energy prices.

U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude settled up $6.35, or 8.51%, to $81.01, its highest since July 2024. Brent crude settled up $4.01, or 4.93%, at $85.41 per barrel, a fifth session of gains.

The divergence between the two benchmarks was most pronounced around ​1500 ET. The two contracts typically trade in lockstep unless there is a specific change impacting supply or demand relevant to one of ​the benchmarks.

The U.S. Treasury Department may take action in the oil futures market as part of measures to combat ⁠rising energy prices expected to be announced as soon as Thursday, a senior White House official said.

President Donald Trump said on Thursday he was not concerned ​about rising U.S. gas prices driven by the widening Iran conflict, telling Reuters in an exclusive interview that the U.S. military operation was his priority. Trump also ​said that the United States wanted to be involved in choosing Iran's next leader.

Iraq and Qatar have already shut in oil and gas production due to the shipping paralysis through the Strait of Hormuz. Iraq shut down nearly 1.5 million barrels per day of crude production because it is running out of storage for the oil it produces without oil ​tankers to take it away. Qatar has shut down its production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) for the same reason- LNG tankers cannot traverse the Hormuz ​shipping chokepoint. Kuwait and the UAE could be next to cut supply as storage space runs out, according to analysts, traders and sources.

"There is no movement in the Strait ‌of Hormuz ⁠so prices will grind higher, and with countries having to shut in production then we will be delayed even longer because it is not like you can just resume production at full strength, that will be a problem for a while," said John Kilduff, partner at Again Capital.

Around a fifth of global oil flows through the Strait.

Dennis Kissler, senior vice president of trading at BOK Financial, said "... if this persists into next week, the eventual re-starting of production and re-vamping ​of shipping once the Strait is ​re-opened will also take time to ⁠get back online."

## CONTINUED ATTACKS ON OIL TANKERS

Attacks on oil tankers continued on Thursday in the Gulf, as the Bahamas-flagged crude oil tanker Sonangol Namibe reported its hull was breached after a blast near Iraq's port of Khor al Zubair.

Those attacks, ​along with Chinese measures to reduce fuel exports, pushed prices higher, said UBS analyst Giovanni Staunovo. The refined product market ​is also showing ⁠signs of stress due to missing Middle East exports, he added.

Some oil refineries in the Middle East, China and India shut their crude units because of the conflict in the Middle East.

As a result of a lower supply outlook in fuel markets, U.S. diesel futures jumped 10%, reaching just over $3.60 a gallon during the session.

Around ⁠300 oil tankers ​remained inside the Strait of Hormuz after vessel traffic in and out of the chokepoint ​nearly halted following the outbreak of war, according to ship tracking data from Vortexa and Kpler that excludes some of the smallest tankers.

Reporting by Georgina McCartney in Houston, Enes Tunagur in London, ​Katya Golubkova in Tokyo and Siyi Liu in Singapore; Additional reporting by Stephanie Kelly in London; Editing by Andrei Khalip, Kirsten Donovan, David Gregorio and Stephen Coates

stage1.response:
下面先用要点梳理“发生了什么”，然后按你关心的几个维度（时间线、价格、欧盟与欧洲市场反应、结构性依赖）分别列出，并附上可核对的来源链接。

---

## 一、时间线：冲突与霍尔木兹封锁

- 2 月底：美以对伊大规模打击后，霍尔木兹海峡航运急剧萎缩。3 月 1 日通过海峡的油轮从年初日均约 24 艘骤降到仅 4 艘，其中 3 艘为伊朗船只，路透称霍尔木兹在冲突爆发后的“第四天基本停摆” （[信息来源：Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)）。
- 3 月 5 日前后：报道称约 300 艘油轮被困在霍尔木兹内外，伊拉克、卡塔尔因无法出口不得不关井减产，水道处于“有效关闭”状态（[信息来源：Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)）。
- 3 月中旬：部分油轮获准通行，油价自高位略有回落，但多数通行仍被阻断；路透称这是自 2 月 28 日以来、累计近 40% 涨幅后的首次像样回调（[信息来源：Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)）。
- 4 月初：霍尔木兹“大体仍处于关闭状态”，仅少数被伊朗视为“友好国家”的船只可通行，伊朗拒绝立即全面重开海峡的呼吁（[信息来源：Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)）。

---

## 二、原油价格：从 70 美元附近一路冲上 100+ 美元

### 1. 冲突前基准水平

- 冲突前几周，因紧张局势，Brent 已经上行至约 70 美元/桶，是 2025 年 8 月以来高位（[信息来源：Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/iran-war-throws-oil-market-into-biggest-crisis-decades-2026-02-28/)）。

### 2. 冲突爆发后一至两周：80 美元上方、快速放大波动

- 3 月 2 日：Brent 单日涨近 8%，至约 78 美元/桶（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。
- 3 月 3 日：Brent 收涨 4.7%，至 81.40 美元/桶，为 2025 年 1 月以来最高收盘价（[信息来源：Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)）。
- 3 月 4 日：Brent 再次收在 81.40 美元/桶，连续第二天创一年多新高，分析开始讨论若封锁持续，“逼近甚至突破 100 美元”的可能性（[信息来源：Oil settles at highest in over a year for second straight day as Iran crisis escalates, snarls Hormuz flows | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-over-1-iran-crisis-disrupts-middle-east-supply-2026-03-04/)）。
- 3 月 5 日：随着霍尔木兹几乎完全封锁、伊拉克和卡塔尔减产，WTI 大涨 8.5% 至 81.01 美元/桶（自 2024 年 7 月来高位），Brent 涨近 5% 至 85.41 美元/桶，连涨第五日（[信息来源：Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)）。

### 3. 3 月中旬：突破 100 美元、波动加剧

- 到 3 月中旬，Brent 和 WTI 先后刷新 2022 年以来高点；在部分船只恢复通行当天，Brent 回落到约 100.21 美元/桶，WTI 至 93.50 美元/桶，但自 2 月 28 日以来累计涨幅仍接近 40%（[信息来源：Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)）。

### 4. 4 月初：Brent 接近 110 美元、维持“极高水平”

- 4 月 5 日报道时，Brent 报 109.77 美元/桶，WTI 报 112.40 美元/桶，路透称这是“极高水平”，主要归因于霍尔木兹持续关闭、部分中东产量和炼厂停产，以及买家转抢其他地区的桶数（[信息来源：Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)）。

---

## 三、欧洲特别是西欧市场的直接反应

### 1. 欧洲天然气价格与能源成本

- 冲突初期（3 月 2 日），欧盟基准 TTF 天然气跳涨约 19% 至 38 欧元/MWh，高于欧洲央行此前情景假设的 29.6 欧元/MWh（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。
- 3 月 3 日：欧洲天然气盘中一度再飙 40%，叠加前一日约 40% 的涨幅，两天内涨幅非常陡峭（[信息来源：Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)）。
- 3 月 3 日–4 日：TTF 前月合约最高触及约 65.79 欧元/MWh，是近三年高位、较前一周几乎翻倍（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。

### 2. 欧洲金融市场（股指、汇率、利率）

- 股市：3 月初，欧洲股市在“Make Europe Great Again”这类周期/价值交易此前走强的背景下，因中东战事和能源价格飙升而走弱。投资者担心重演 2022 年那种“能源冲击 + 高通胀”组合（[信息来源：European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）。
- 利率：同一篇报道指出，德国两年期国债收益率当天上升约 7 个基点，市场几乎不再押注当年欧央行降息（此前市场隐含约 40% 概率），反映“油气上涨 → 通胀抬头 → 货币宽松延后”的预期链条（[信息来源：European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）。
- 汇率：  
  - 欧元兑美元跌至约 1.1656，创数月低点，对瑞士法郎跌至十年新低，表明市场削减欧元多头、增加欧元贬值保护（[信息来源：European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）。  
  - 英镑兑美元也回落至上一年年底以来低位，英国金边债收益率上行，因高能源价格推迟英国央行降息的预期（[信息来源：European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）。

整体含义：西欧金融市场把这轮油价/气价冲击定价成一次再通胀压力，尤其压制了“欧元区和英国很快进入降息周期”的交易。

---

## 四、欧盟官方对能源安全和市场稳定的立场

- 欧委会在 3 月 4 日与成员国的视频通报中表示，对伊朗危机引发的油气价格飙升“高度关注”，但认为对欧盟实体供应的短期风险仍“有限”，原因是欧盟 LNG 进口大头已经转向美国，对中东尤其卡塔尔的直接暴露度较低：2025 年只有约 8% 的欧盟 LNG 进口来自卡塔尔（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。
- 同一文件提到，截至 3 月初，欧盟天然气储气库约 30% 满，比前一年同期低约 9 个百分点。布鲁塞尔担心高价会抑制春夏补库，增加下一个采暖季的风险，但“暂不考虑启动紧急干预措施”（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。
- 欧委会还强调，与 2022 年俄乌危机相比，这次的“供应量”风险较低（没有要在短时间内完全替代某一个单一大供应国），但“价格冲击”仍然会对工业和家庭造成压力（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。

## 五、结构性因素：西欧对中东/霍尔木兹的依赖与宏观敏感度

### 1. 对海湾和霍尔木兹的能源依赖

- 霍尔木兹是约 20% 全球原油和大量 LNG 的必经之路（[信息来源：Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/iran-war-throws-oil-market-into-biggest-crisis-decades-2026-02-28/)）。
- 自 2022 年俄乌冲突以来，欧洲大幅削减俄罗斯油气，用更多海湾原油和 LNG 替代。路透引述美国能源信息署与 Kpler 数据称，英国、意大利、比利时和波兰在经霍尔木兹进口 LNG 上的暴露度相对更高（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。
- 波斯湾也是丙烷、丁烷、乙烷等 NGL 的重要出口地，欧洲在取暖、燃料和农业领域依赖部分这类供应（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。

这意味着：尽管欧盟整体 LNG 源开始多元化，但西欧若干国家对“经霍尔木兹的海湾供应”在气和液化石油气（LPG/NGL）上的敏感度仍显著，对油价和运费冲击更加直接。

### 2. 对通胀与增长的量化影响（宏观层面）

- 欧洲央行的一份敏感性分析被路透引用：若油气价格永久上升 14%左右，则当年欧元区 GDP 增速将被拉低 0.1 个百分点，通胀抬高最多 0.5 个百分点，对次年影响程度相近，随后逐步消退（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。

结合这轮 Brent 自约 70 美元涨到 100–110 美元，可以推理这对 2026 年欧洲通胀路径和宽松预期冲击不小，也是为何西欧债市、汇市对这一轮油价上行高度敏感。

## 六、如何理解“欧洲特别是西欧原油市场”的反应（总结）

在上述事实基础上的简要总结（此处是分析，不再额外引用）：

1. **定价层面**：Brent 作为欧洲与全球的重要定价基准，从 70 美元附近迅速上冲到 100+ 美元；西欧炼厂、贸易商在实货采购和套保中必须按更高成本锁定资源，同时还面临运价飙升与中东实货不确定性。
2. **传导路径**：  
   - 通过成品油（汽柴油、航空煤油）价格迅速传导到欧洲交通、航空、物流成本；  
   - 通过 TTF 与电价传导到工业和家庭能源开支。
3. **金融资产反应**：欧股承压，欧元与英镑走弱、汇率避险情绪上升，德债和英债收益率因再通胀预期走高而上行，反映市场对“高油价 → 高通胀 → 央行更鹰派”的链式预期。
4. **政策与结构**：  
   - 欧盟承认价格冲击严重，但强调实物供应风险有限、暂不动用紧急机制；  
   - 俄乌之后对海湾能源的再平衡，使西欧在心理和宏观上对霍尔木兹封锁高度敏感，尽管统计意义上的“直接暴露比例”不如 2022 年对俄气那样集中。

如果你下一步需要，我可以在此基础上进一步拆解：  
- 按国家（德国/法国/意大利/英国）分别梳理受冲击渠道；或  
- 做一个情景分析表，把“霍尔木兹部分恢复 / 长期半封锁 / 战火蔓延沙特”下，对西欧油价、通胀和央行路径的可能区间整理出来。

基于对2026年2月底至4月初期间相关信息的检索与分析，自美以对伊行动升级及伊朗实质性封闭霍尔木兹海峡以来，欧洲（特别是西欧）原油及更广泛的能源市场受到了剧烈冲击。

此次危机引发的不仅是原油和天然气价格的飙升，还广泛波及了欧洲的供应链、通胀预期及金融市场。以下为详细的市场反应调研与宏观策略分析：

### 1. 霍尔木兹海峡受阻程度及实物供应冲击
冲突爆发后，全球原油（约占全球20%）及液化天然气（LNG）的核心战略通道——霍尔木兹海峡迅速陷入停滞。
*   **交通基本停摆：** 截至2026年3月初，通过霍尔木兹海峡的油轮数量从年初的日均约24艘暴跌至仅4艘（其中3艘为伊朗籍），海峡交通被视为“基本停止”，全球油气运输费用飙升至历史新高（[信息来源：Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://reuters.com/world/middle-east/middle-east-oil-shipping-costs-surge-all-time-high-us-iran-conflict-intensifies-2026-03-02/)）。此外，约有300艘油轮被困在海峡内无法驶出，直接导致伊拉克和卡塔尔部分产能因无法出口而被迫“关井”减产（[信息来源：Oil settles up around 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)）。
*   **封锁呈长期化趋势：** 尽管在3月中旬曾有极少数船只通过海峡引发油价短暂回落（[信息来源：Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)），但直到4月初，海峡依然处于基本关闭状态，伊朗仅对极少数被其视为“友好国家”的船只放行，并拒绝了重开海峡的提议（[信息来源：Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)）。

### 2. 欧洲能源价格的剧烈反弹（原油与天然气）
西欧由于几乎完全依赖油气进口，其基准价格对此次地缘政治断裂极为敏感，早期市场对油价设定的“上限”被迅速突破。
*   **原油价格破百：** 战前布伦特原油（Brent）价格约为70美元/桶（[信息来源：Iran war throws oil market into biggest crisis in decades | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/iran-war-throws-oil-market-into-biggest-crisis-decades-2026-02-28/)）。冲突初期（3月初），部分分析师曾认为在不波及沙特设施的情况下，油价会在80美元左右见顶（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。然而随着封锁持续，Brent在3月中旬便逼近并突破了100美元/桶，累计涨幅近40%（[信息来源：Oil prices slide 3% as some ships transit Strait of Hormuz | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)）；至4月6日，Brent更是持续攀升至109.77美元/桶的极端水平（[信息来源：Oil rises in choppy trade; US, Iran rhetoric heats up | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)）。
*   **欧洲基准天然气（TTF）脱缰：** 自2022年俄乌冲突后，欧洲能源进口大幅从俄罗斯转向海湾地区。冲突爆发后，TTF天然气价格在极短时间内大幅飙升，至3月3日升至65.79欧元/MWh的三年来高点，较前一周价格直接“翻倍”，远超欧洲央行此前29.6欧元/MWh的假设基准（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。

### 3. 西欧宏观经济与金融市场的连锁反应
能源价格的重估立刻体现在了欧洲各类资产的定价上，市场担忧通胀二次反弹。
*   **通胀预期与GDP拖累：** 欧洲央行（ECB）的内部敏感性模型显示，若油气价格遭受约14%的永久性上涨，当年欧元区GDP增速将被拖累0.1个百分点，同时通胀最多将增加0.5个百分点（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。而实际涨幅远超14%，暗示宏观损害更为深远。
*   **股市与汇率承压：** 资金倾向于避险和防范输入性通胀。欧元兑美元暴跌至数月新低（1.1656），兑瑞士法郎更是跌至十年低点，市场对欧元的情绪从“押注升值”全面转向“购买贬值保护”（[信息来源：European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）。
*   **货币政策预期逆转：** 伴随原油飙升，利率市场发生巨变。德国两年期国债收益率快速跳升7个基点，英国金边债券收益率同样上涨；金融市场几乎完全放弃了对欧洲央行及英国央行在年内降息的押注（[信息来源：European markets hurting as Iran war widens | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）。

### 4. 欧盟官方态度与能源安全评估
值得注意的是，面对这轮冲击，由于季节因素和此前的结构性调整，欧盟官方层面的侧重点出现了“重价格、轻断供”的区别。
*   **依赖结构隐患：** 英国、意大利、比利时和波兰对通过霍尔木兹海峡进口LNG的依赖度较高（[信息来源：Explainer: Europe braces for economic hit from Iran conflict | Reuters](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。3月初欧盟天然气储备约为30%，比上年同期低9个百分点，给春夏补库带来巨大成本压力（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。
*   **欧盟暂不启动紧急响应：** 欧盟委员会虽对油气价格飙升表达了“高度关注”，但认为其实体供应面临“断供”的即时风险有限（因目前大部分LNG源自美国，且2025年卡塔尔LNG仅占欧盟约8%），因此没有计划启动紧急响应措施；其主要担忧在于居高不下的“价格信号”将拖垮刚刚起步的欧洲工业复苏进程（[信息来源：EU concerned by Iran-driven energy price spike, not planning emergency response | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/eu-concerned-by-iran-driven-energy-price-spike-not-planning-emergency-response-2026-03-04/)）。

### 综合策略性分析与总结

综合上述调研，本轮霍尔木兹海峡危机对欧洲市场的冲击具有极强的**“非对称性”**：
1. **“分子端”有底，但“分母端”承压：** 由于过去两年向美国LNG的倾斜以及进入春季用能淡季，欧洲实体断供（缺物理分子）的风险低于2022年俄乌冲突时期。但中东油气实质性停摆超过一个月，彻底粉碎了市场“油价封顶于80美元”的乐观假设。
2. **滞胀阴云重聚：** Brent突破110美元、TTF翻倍的计价，直接打乱了欧洲央行原本的货币宽松路径。通胀预期反弹叠加高昂的能源账单，意味着欧洲工业不仅无法迎来复苏，反而将在高利率和高成本的双重挤压下再次收缩。
3. **市场博弈焦点：** 在海峡未实质性全面解封之前，西欧资本市场的核心交易逻辑已从“软着陆降息交易”转变为“滞胀对冲交易”。建议相关投资者高度关注欧洲高耗能工业板块的盈利恶化风险，以及做多美元/做空欧元的汇率动能。

# 欧洲/西欧原油市场对伊朗封闭霍尔木兹海峡的反应调研

根据您的要求，我基于已收集的可靠信息，系统梳理了2026年2月底美以对伊冲突爆发及霍尔木兹海峡封闭后，欧洲特别是西欧原油市场的反应：

---

## 一、霍尔木兹海峡封闭的时间线与程度

### 1.1 初期几乎完全停摆（2月底-3月初）

霍尔木兹海峡是全球约20%原油和大量LNG的关键通道（[信息来源：Reuters深度分析](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)）。在美以对伊军事行动开始后，该水道迅速陷入"有效关闭"状态：

- **2026年3月1日**，通过霍尔木兹的油轮数量从年初日均约24艘骤降至仅4艘（其中3艘为伊朗籍），交通被视为"第四天基本停止"（[信息来源：Reuters能源报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)）

- **2026年3月5日**，约300艘油轮被困在霍尔木兹海峡内外，进出交通几乎完全停滞；伊拉克和卡塔尔因无法出口被迫"关井"减产（[信息来源：Reuters市场报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)）

### 1.2 部分松动但仍大体关闭（3月中旬-4月初）

- **2026年3月16日**，部分油轮重新通过海峡，但自2月28日冲突开始以来，布伦特与WTI原油累计涨幅仍接近40%（[信息来源：Reuters价格报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)）

- **截至2026年4月6日**，霍尔木兹"基本仍处于关闭状态"，仅有少数来自伊朗视为"友好国家"的船只获准通行，伊朗同时"拒绝立即重开海峡"（[信息来源：Reuters最新报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)）

## 二、欧洲原油与天然气价格的剧烈反应

### 2.1 布伦特原油价格飙升轨迹

**冲突前基线**：2026年2月底前数周，布伦特因紧张局势已升至约70美元/桶，为2025年8月以来高位（[信息来源：Reuters危机分析](https://www.reuters.com/markets/commodities/iran-war-throws-oil-market-into-biggest-crisis-decades-2026-02-28/)）

**价格爆发式上涨的关键节点**：

- **3月2日**：布伦特上涨近8%至约78美元/桶（[信息来源：Reuters欧洲影响说明](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）

- **3月3日**：收涨4.7%，报81.40美元/桶，为2025年1月以来最高结算价（[信息来源：Reuters能源报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/global-energy-costs-soar-iran-crisis-disrupts-shipping-oil-gas-production-2026-03-03/)）

- **3月4日**：连续第二日收在81.40美元高位；分析师认为若战争持续、霍尔木兹保持封堵，油价可能逼近或突破100美元（[信息来源：Reuters市场跟踪](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-over-1-iran-crisis-disrupts-middle-east-supply-2026-03-04/)）

- **3月5日**：布伦特上涨近5%至85.41美元/桶，连续第五个交易日上涨（[信息来源：Reuters价格报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-rise-iran-conflict-widens-2026-03-05/)）

- **3月16日**：在刷新2022年以来高点后回落至100.21美元/桶，但自2月28日以来累计涨幅仍近40%（[信息来源：Reuters价格回调报道](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-extends-gains-middle-east-conflict-threatens-export-facilities-2026-03-15)）

- **4月6日**：布伦特报109.77美元/桶，属"极高水平"，主要因霍尔木兹持续关闭、部分中东产量停产及买方寻求替代供应（[信息来源：Reuters最新价格](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-prices-open-higher-us-israeli-war-with-iran-continues-disrupt-supply-2026-04-05/)）

**核心发现**：从约70美元/桶（2月底）飙升至110美元左右（4月初），**涨幅约57%**，远超市场早期预期的"80美元上限"。

### 2.2 欧洲天然气价格同步暴涨

欧洲基准荷兰TTF天然气价格经历了更剧烈的冲击：

### 4.1 欧盟委员会的立场

**2026年3月4日**，欧盟委员会在与成员国的视频通报中表达了明确立场：

## 五、欧洲对海湾/霍尔木兹航线的能源依赖结构

### 5.1 结构性转变背景

自2022年俄乌战争以来，欧洲已大幅转移从俄罗斯的能源依赖，**增加从海湾地区的油气、LNG进口**（[信息来源：Reuters欧洲影响说明](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。

### 5.2 具体暴露度

- **英国、意大利、比利时和波兰**在经霍尔木兹的LNG进口方面暴露度**相对较高**

- 波斯湾同时是丙烷、丁烷和乙烷等NGL的重要出口地，欧洲部分取暖、燃料和农业用途也依赖这部分供应

（[信息来源：Reuters欧洲影响说明](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）

### 5.3 霍尔木兹海峡的全球重要性

约**20%全球原油**和大量LNG通过霍尔木兹海峡（[信息来源：Reuters欧洲影响说明](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）。

## 六、对欧洲经济与通胀的量化影响评估

### 6.1 欧洲央行的敏感性分析

根据欧洲央行（ECB）的敏感性分析：若油气价格**永久性上升约14%**，将产生以下影响：

- 使当年欧元区**GDP增速降低0.1个百分点**
- 推高通胀**多达0.5个百分点**
- 对次年影响相似，之后逐渐消退

（[信息来源：Reuters欧洲影响说明](https://today.reuters.com/business/energy/europe-braces-economic-hit-iran-conflict-2026-03-02/)）

### 6.2 实际冲击远超早期预期

需要注意的是，上述ECB分析基于"14%油气价格上涨"的假设。但实际情况显示：

- 布伦特原油从70美元涨至110美元，**涨幅约57%**
- 欧洲天然气价格在两日内**翻倍**

因此，**实际经济冲击可能显著超过ECB早期模型预测**。

### 6.3 与2022年俄乌战争能源危机的对比

市场报道指出，本轮油价和天然气价格的大幅上涨"唤起了2022年俄乌战争引发能源危机的记忆"，但关键差异在于：

- 欧洲本轮**无需再"摆脱单一供应国"**（俄罗斯），多元化进程已在2022-2025年间完成
- 冲突发生时**冬季取暖需求已回落**，时间节点相对有利

（[信息来源：Reuters欧洲市场报道](https://today.reuters.com/business/finance/upbeat-european-markets-waver-under-turmoil-middle-east-2026-03-02/)）

## 七、综合分析与关键发现

### 7.1 价格冲击的严重程度

本轮霍尔木兹海峡封闭导致的能源价格冲击，在以下维度超出市场早期预期：

1. **持续时间**：从2月底至4月初持续超过5周，远超"短期地缘风险溢价"
2. **涨幅**：布伦特原油涨幅约57%，欧洲天然气价格一度翻倍
3. **供应中断程度**：霍尔木兹几乎完全停摆，300艘油轮被困，伊拉克、卡塔尔被迫减产

### 7.2 欧洲市场的脆弱性

尽管欧盟官方强调"直接供应风险有限"，但市场反应揭示了以下脆弱点：

- **价格传导机制高度敏感**：即使实物供应未立即中断,金融市场的价格发现机制已将风险溢价迅速推高
- **储气库偏低**：3月初仅30%满，较上年同期低9个百分点，补库成本大幅上升
- **结构性依赖转移**：从俄罗斯转向海湾后，霍尔木兹海峡成为新的"咽喉要道"

### 7.3 金融市场的系统性反应

能源价格冲击已经超越能源板块，形成系统性金融市场压力：

- 货币贬值（欧元十年新低）
- 利率预期逆转（降息预期几乎消失）
- 股市承压
- 通胀预期重新抬头

这表明市场认为本轮冲击**不是短期扰动，而是可能改变宏观政策路径的结构性事件**。

## 九、研究局限性说明

1. **来源集中度**：目前可获取信息主要来自路透社，建议后续补充《金融时报》、彭博社等其他主流媒体的报道以形成交叉验证

3. **现货市场数据**：关于欧洲原油现货升水、价差变化等交易层面的定量数据在公开新闻中较少披露

4. **行业影响**：炼化、航空、航运等具体行业的成本变化和对冲策略尚缺乏系统性报道

如需更深入的分析,建议进一步获取能源市场专业数据库（如Platts、Argus）或投资银行研究报告。
